mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained output capacity, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC over a 24-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations force central banks to restrict liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 55% of historical instances, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicate persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, invalidating the supply bottleneck risk premium.
